core_mechanism: polar (amplitude-phase) decomposition of a fluctuating complex field into a fixed-phase factor times a fluctuating non-negative magnitude, so the entire hierarchy of moments/correlation functions is determined by the probability distribution of that single magnitude variable, and the first-moment (coherent) amplitude captures only a distribution-set fraction of the mean squared modulus
brief_reason: The abstract decomposes the mode operator into phase and density, assumes a well-defined phase with fluctuating density, and derives that the full correlation-function hierarchy follows from the density statistics while the squared mean amplitude equals only (pi/4) of the total density.
